pages.text: One bright morning, <name> packed her little red bag with a sandwich and a bottle of mango juice. “I’m going to explore the forest near our village!” she told Amma.

The forest was full of chirp-chirp birds and rustle-rustle leaves. Sunshine danced on the ground as <name> skipped along a soft path. Suddenly, a big blue butterfly fluttered by. “Wait for me!” giggled <name>, following it past tall trees and tiny flowers.

Soon, she heard a chatter-chatter sound. A playful monkey swung down from a branch. “Hello! Want to play?” he said. <name> laughed. “Yes! But I’m looking for something shiny I saw under that big tree.”
Together they searched. Under a pile of leaves, they found a small golden bell! It jingled softly — ting-ting!

“It’s so pretty!” said <name>. The monkey smiled. “It belongs to the forest wind. When it rings, everyone smiles.” <name> gently hung the bell on a low branch. The breeze blew, and the bell sang — ting-ting-ting!
All the animals — the birds, squirrels, and butterflies — gathered around, listening happily. <name> clapped her hands. “Now the forest has a new song!”
When she returned home, she told Amma, “The forest shared its secret with me — music and friendship!” That night, the wind outside whispered softly, ting-ting! — like it was saying goodnight.
